name: BASMAH
mission: When you brighten a life, you ignite change that extends far beyond one person. Each individual touched goes on to inspire and uplift those around them, creating a ripple effect of hope and transformation. At BASMAH, we are committed to brightening as many lives as possible—because every life changed brings the world one step closer to a brighter future.
theoryOfChange: The organization states it focuses on sustainable development to empower communities, emphasizing that short-term help is needed but long-term stability is the goal, and aims to enable people to help themselves and their communities.
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://basmah-bd.org/zakat (Source: https://basmah-bd.org/zakat)

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a zakat-eligible entity addressing urgent humanitarian crises [1]. Its mission to empower communities through long-term stability resonates with donors seeking high-urgency cause areas and direct intervention models [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: BASMAH focuses on direct service delivery through medical and educational infrastructure, though its impact score reflects a need for more robust evidence of cost-effectiveness per beneficiary [2][3]. The organization currently holds 0.8 months of working capital, indicating a tight operational budget for its ongoing programs [3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Bangladesh American Society of Muslim Aid for Humanity Inc (BASMAH) provides critical humanitarian relief and long-term developmental support to Rohingya refugees and vulnerable communities in Bangladesh.
strengths: Direct service provider for high-urgency humanitarian needs among Rohingya refugees
Maintains zakat eligibility with clear policy alignment for Muslim donors
Offers diverse giving methods including support for orphans and medical aid
baseline_narrative.summary: BASMAH operates specialized medical centers, educational facilities, and orphan centers designed to transition beneficiaries from short-term aid to long-term stability [2][4]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various humanitarian programs [1].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its high alignment with humanitarian causes and donor needs, though its financial health is constrained by low working capital reserves.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization currently maintains only <cite id="9">0.8 months of working capital</cite>, which creates significant continuity risk in the event of a funding downturn.
While BASMAH tracks <cite id="10">one outcome metric via Candid</cite>, it lacks the multi-year longitudinal data or external evaluations needed for a top-tier impact score.

bbb_assessment.summary: BASMAH is a <cite id="11">BBB Accredited Charity</cite>, meeting all 20 standards for charity accountability.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's rapid revenue growth may eventually allow it to build the necessary reserves if managed conservatively.

<cite id="15">Incomplete expense breakdown data</cite> resulting in a 0% reported program ratio
case_against.summary: The primary concern is financial sustainability; the organization has <cite id="9">less than one month of cash reserves</cite> and incomplete data regarding its program expense ratio.

confidence.notes: Transparency is high, but financial health metrics show significant risk.

alignment.explanation: Alignment is very high due to <cite id="5">explicit zakat programs</cite> and a focus on <cite id="1">high-urgency humanitarian needs</cite> in Muslim-majority regions.
alignment.improvement: The organization could further improve by <cite id="14">expanding services to other underserved geographies</cite> beyond its current focus areas.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust BASMAH due to its <cite id="6">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and its status as a <cite id="11">BBB Accredited Charity</cite>, which confirms adherence to 20 standards of accountability.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="12">expand its board size</cite> beyond the current three members to ensure more robust independent oversight.

impact.explanation: The impact score is driven by a <cite id="3">direct service delivery model</cite> and a clear theory of change, though it is hampered by <cite id="9">low working capital</cite>.
impact.improvement: BASMAH should <cite id="13">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for the calculation of cost-per-beneficiary metrics.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Measured outcomes with explicit Theory of Change

rich_narrative.headline: The Bangladesh American Society of Muslim Aid for Humanity Inc (BASMAH) provides critical humanitarian relief and long-term developmental support to Rohingya refugees and vulnerable communities in Bangladesh.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support direct, faith-aligned humanitarian relief for Rohingya refugees through a rapidly growing organization with high transparency ratings.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through a verified organization serving multiple asnaf
Prioritize direct service delivery over funding large international intermediaries
Value high transparency as evidenced by the Candid Platinum seal
Care specifically about the Rohingya refugee crisis in Bangladesh
giving_considerations: The organization is in a high-growth phase with limited financial reserves
Detailed cost-effectiveness data is currently unavailable
Gifts support direct operations like medical centers and schools
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors who require organizations to have at least 6-12 months of operating reserves or those seeking long-term academic impact evaluations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that outcomes are <cite id="10">measured and tracked via Candid</cite>, but no external or independent evaluations are yet available.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: BASMAH aims to empower individuals through <cite id="10">better opportunities and long-term stability</cite>, moving beyond short-term aid to self-sufficiency.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that direct services like medical aid are actually improving long-term health outcomes for refugees.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The ongoing Rohingya crisis and BASMAH's <cite id="2">42.1% growth rate</cite> suggest a high capacity to absorb additional funding for program expansion.

peer_comparison.differentiator: BASMAH is distinguished by its <cite id="2">42.1% revenue growth rate</cite>, which significantly outpaces many established peers in the humanitarian sector.

strengths.detail: The organization has demonstrated a remarkable <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 42.1%</cite>, indicating strong fundraising momentum and increasing donor confidence.
BASMAH has earned the <cite id="6">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>, the highest level of recognition for nonprofit disclosure.
Unlike many intermediaries, BASMAH utilizes a <cite id="3">direct service model</cite> to provide medical aid and education directly to beneficiaries in Bangladesh.

strengths_deep_dive: BASMAH's operational strength lies in its ability to provide <cite id="3">direct humanitarian relief</cite> in high-urgency environments. By managing its own facilities, the organization ensures that aid reaches <cite id="1">Rohingya refugees</cite> without the overhead of multiple sub-partners. This efficiency, combined with a <cite id="5">clear zakat policy</cite> that covers six of the eight eligible categories, makes it a highly attractive option for Muslim donors seeking high-impact, faith-aligned giving.
rich_narrative.summary: The Bangladesh American Society of Muslim Aid for Humanity Inc (BASMAH) is a humanitarian organization dedicated to serving <cite id="1">Rohingya refugees and vulnerable communities in Bangladesh</cite>. founded in 2018, the organization has experienced rapid growth, achieving a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 42.1%</cite> as it scales its response to one of the world's most pressing refugee crises. BASMAH operates through a direct service model, managing <cite id="3">specialized medical centers, educational facilities, and orphan centers</cite> that aim to transition beneficiaries from emergency relief to long-term stability. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="4">total revenue of $4,195,860</cite>, reflecting significant donor trust and expanded operational capacity. A key differentiator for BASMAH is its deep integration with Islamic giving principles; it maintains a <cite id="5">verified zakat-eligible status</cite> and serves multiple asnaf categories, including the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin). While the organization has achieved a <cite id="6">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, it remains in a growth phase, focusing on expanding its <cite id="7">WASH (water, sanitation, and hygiene) and empowerment programs</cite> to meet the ongoing needs of displaced populations.
